introduction: As we consider transitioning from dealership franchising to a direct-to-customer sales model, it's crucial to understand the potential benefits. This analysis outlines the expected impact on sales and profit margins by 2027.

subsections.heading: Current State of Franchise Dealerships
subsections.content:
• Dealerships have been identified as adding significant markups to the manufacturer's suggested retail price, with additional fees potentially reaching up to $20,000 per vehicle.
• The traditional dealership model has led to annual revenues of around $100 billion for the top 10 dealership groups in America.
• In contrast, Elexion's 2024 market study highlighted the effectiveness of online engagement, demonstrating higher lead conversion rates for customers using an online configurator than in-person dealership visits.
• The digital-first approach to sales could reduce overhead costs related to dealership maintenance and operations.
• The shift to an online model could also mitigate the unnecessary markups and fees associated with traditional dealerships, potentially boosting margins.
• This could also create an opportunity for Elexion to redefine its sales strategies by leveraging data-driven insights from online customer interactions.
subsections.justification: The content presents crucial data about the inefficiencies of traditional dealership models and the potential advantages of a direct-to-customer model, helping answer the DR question.

subsections.heading: Direct-to-Customer Model: Potential Benefits
subsections.content:
• Direct sales models can potentially reduce overhead costs related to dealership maintenance
• It could allow for greater control over pricing and profit margins
• Direct-to-Customer sales can provide an opportunity to strengthen customer relationships
• This model can offer greater flexibility in inventory management
• It could allow Elexion to have a more uniform brand representation
• Direct sales models can potentially reduce the time taken to bring a vehicle to market
subsections.justification: While this content discusses potential benefits of a direct sales model, it doesn't specifically address strategies to drive sales and higher margins in 2027.

subsections.heading: Strategies for Maximizing Sales & Margins
subsections.content:
• Implementing advanced analytics to predict sales trends and optimize pricing
• Expanding the product portfolio to cater to different consumer segments
• Increasing the efficiency of manufacturing processes to lower production costs
• Investing in marketing and branding activities to boost product visibility
• Enhancing after-sales service offerings to promote customer retention
• Leveraging partnerships with supply chain partners to reduce procurement costs
• Streamlining operational processes to improve overall productivity
subsections.justification: These are general strategies for sales and margin maximization and do not directly address the transition to a direct-to-customer sales model.

conclusion: Adopting a direct-to-customer model has potential to significantly enhance our sales performance and profit margins. As we move forward, it's crucial to develop strategies that leverage these benefits effectively.
